kept.text:
Trend sürerken sahadan gelen verileri takip ettiğime artık aşina oldunuz. TSMC'nin en gelişmiş çip üretim hattında yapay zeka çiplerinin payı 2025'te yüzde 9'ken 2026'da yüzde 60'a, 2027'de yüzde 90'a çıkıyor. İki yıl içinde bu üretim hattının neredeyse tamamı yapay zekaya ait olacak. Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, Amazon ve Google hepsi aynı hat için yarışıyor, yapay zeka dışındaki her şey telefon çipleri oyun konsolları araç çipleri yavaş yavaş dışarı itiliyor.

2026 ikinci yarısında bu hattın kapasite kullanımı yüzde 100'ü aşması bekleniyor yani talep fiziksel üretim kapasitesinden fazla. TSMC yeni fabrikalar inşa ediyor ama çip fabrikası kurmak yıllar sürüyor ve milyarlarca dolar gerektiriyor. Kısa vadede bu sıkışıklığı çözecek bir şey yok. Yapay zeka altyapısındaki darboğazların neden devam edeceğini merak edenler bu grafiğe baksın, her şey bu üretim hattından başlıyor ve bu hat dolmak üzere.

profile_bio.description:
Turning macro noise into market clarity
B. Sc. Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich Computer Science & Economics
İstanbul Erkek Lisesi'21

quoted_tweet.text:
Sahadan gelen güncel veriler DRAM segmentindeki ivmenin kesintisiz bir şekilde devam ettiğini ve sektördeki yükseliş trendinin gücünü net bir şekilde gösteriyor. Trend devamlılığını kontrol etmek adına grafik dışında kritik öneme sahip olan ihracat rakamlarını  ve ürün fiyatlarını incelemek bence çok ciddi bir ek konfirmasyon sağlıyor.

İlk grafiğe baktığımızda DRAM ihracatının aylık bazda %15 ve çeyreklik bazda %83 gibi devasa bir artışla 9,25 milyar dolara ulaştığı görülüyor. Bu hacim artışına eşlik eden SSD tarafındaki %181'lik çeyreklik büyüme ve MCP tarafındaki 8,16 milyar dolarlık yüksek seyir, talebin tek bir ürüne değil genele yayıldığını ispatlıyor. 

İkinci grafik ise birim fiyatlardaki dikey yükselişi gösteriyor. DRAM birim ihracat fiyatları Mart ayına kıyasla %26 oranında bir sıçrama gerçekleştirdi yani trendde bir yavaşlama yok aksine fiyat artış hızı git gide artıyor. Hem ihracat miktarının hem de birim fiyatın eş zamanlı olarak yukarı yönlü hareket etmesi, sahadaki iştahın tazeliğini koruduğunu bize gösteriyor. Alıntıladığım tweette söylediğim gibi bu ek konfirmasyonları sık sık aylık bazda kontrol edip trend değişimi görmedikçe pozisyonumu korumaya devam edeceğim

kept.text:
CPOで一番成長率が高く見えやすいのは、まずスイッチASICのすぐ横に載る光エンジン、PIC、光I/Oモジュール」です。これは今の市場規模が小さいので、NVIDIAやBroadcomのCPOスイッチ採用が始まるだけで前年比が異常に大きく出ます。BroadcomはCPOを光とシリコンを同一パッケージ基板上に統合する技術と説明しており、51.2T CPO Ethernet switchの量産やCorningとのファイバー・コネクタ協業も出ています。

次に伸びるのはCPO向けフォトニクス実装です。YoleはCPO向けフォトニクス・パッケージング市場が2031年に50億ドル近くへ拡大すると見ています。ここには光エンジンを作るだけでなく、チップレット周辺の接合、実装、光ファイバー取り付け、熱制御、検査まで入ります。
全く来てませんが、自分が検査を推してるのはこのためです。

ただし、すぐ売上になりやすいのはCPO本体だけではありません。CoherentはCPO向けのSAMに、ELSモジュール、高出力CWレーザー、PIC、fiber attach units、アイソレーター、TEC、偏波保持レンズアレイなどを含めて150億ドルと説明しています。これはCPO市場142%の外側にあるけど、実際のお金は入って来やすい周辺部品です。

@dssdssdssss Diamond hands / big balls strategy: 

Pick 2-4 names max. Something like 2 high-beta names mixed w 2 stable names works quite well.

kept.text:
Win Semi (3105.TWO) just had its best ER in years. The market is not pricing it in

EPS up 30x YoY. Revenue +28% YoY. Q2 guidance up 14-16% QoQ. And that’s with capacity utilization still only at 60%.

The two growth engines going forward: Optical Communications and LEO Satellites.

AI data center revenue expected to more than double this year. Optical PD products entering mass production in Q2 with significant ramp in H2. 4-inch InP line capacity expanding by multiples this year to meet demand.

Win Semi is the largest pure-play compound semiconductor foundry in the world – 65% global market share. Without them, the entire III-V ecosystem grinds to a halt. They make the GaAs, InP, and GaN chips that go into 5G base stations, satellites, defense radar, AI data center optics, EVs, and quantum sensors.

Win Semi is the confirmed outsourced manufacturing partner for Sivers’ DFB lasers and laser arrays – scaling high-volume production for CWDM and DWDM applications, CPO and next-generation optical interconnects.

When $SIVE scales, Win Semi scales with it. 

They are not just Sivers’ foundry. They are the foundry for the entire compound semiconductor universe. WiFi 7 chips for $QCOM. RF front-end modules for $AVGO. EML lasers for hyperscaler transceivers. Power amplifiers for LEO satellite constellations.

When the optical interconnect market goes from $15B to $154B, Win Semi is in the path of every single dollar. There is no scaling photonics without scaling Win Semi.

The market is not fully pricing it in.

Bullish Win Semi

I’m long Win Semi (3105.TWO)

Win Semi just printed one of the best Q1s in years and I don’t hear much talk about it.

EPS up 30x YoY. Revenue +28% YoY. Q2 guidance up 14-16% QoQ. And that’s with capacity utilization still only at 60%.

The two growth engines going forward: Optical Communications and LEO Satellites.

AI data center revenue expected to more than double this year. Optical PD products entering mass production in Q2 with significant ramp in H2. 4-inch InP line capacity expanding by multiples this year to meet demand.

Now here’s why this matters to $SIVE holders specifically.

Win Semi is the confirmed outsourced manufacturing partner for Sivers’ DFB lasers and laser arrays – scaling high-volume production for CWDM and DWDM applications, CPO and next-generation optical interconnects. 

When $SIVE scales, Win Semi scales with it. They’re a foundry that is embedded in the same photonics supply chain feeding AI data centers and LEO satellites.

This is why I love betting on ecosystems rather than single stocks.

Disclosure: I own this stock since @aleabitoreddit called it back in March. It has been one of my favorite stocks on the Taiwanese exchange to add on any macro drop.

🚀 Top 5 Stocks for the Next Decade

The AI revolution is just getting started. 

Here are 5 top stocks to buy for the next decade, powered by massive tailwinds in semiconductors, cloud AI, and next-gen healthcare:
1.  $NVDA – AI GPU kingpin 👑
2.  $AVGO – Custom AI chips, cybersecurity, and more
3.  $TSM – The foundry fueling it all

tweet.text:
WE ARE SO EARLY.

Goldman Sachs just published their CPO supply chain map for $NVDA and $AVGO. It's good but realise how many names are MISSING COMPLETLY. 

$SOI - Not listed anywhere. Goldman maps optical engines, lasers, connectors, FAUs, test equipment but completely skips the substrate layer. Every silicon photonics PIC that TSMC fabs for both NVIDIA and Broadcom's CPO platforms starts on a Soitec Photonics-SOI wafer. That's a glaring omission.

$IQE - No epiwafer layer at all. Goldman jumps from lasers to optical engines without acknowledging who grows the epitaxial layers on those laser chips.

$AIXA - No MOCVD equipment layer. Every III-V laser source listed (Sumitomo, Furukawa, VPEC, LandMark, Lumentum, Coherent) uses AIXTRON or Veeco reactors to grow their material.

$TSE - Goldman lists $TSMC as CPO chip fabrication for both ecosystems but doesn't mention Tower, which is arguably the leading independent silicon photonics foundry and is tripling capacity.

$AEHR - No wafer-level test/burn-in. Goldman lists SPIL for CPO chip test on the NVIDIA side and "TBA" for Broadcom, but misses the wafer-level burn-in step entirely.

$LPK - No glass waveguide or laser processing equipment. Goldman lists Robotechnik and ASMPT for CPO coupling but misses LPKF's LIDE technology for glass-based CPO integration.

$SIVE - Not listed under laser sources despite making InP lasers specifically designed for silicon photonics integration.

I think most people are still playing AI like it’s just a Mag7 trade and that’s where they’re getting it wrong.

the real money shows up in where that spend actually flows..

Every incremental dollar ends up in memory $MU, $SNDK CPUs and custom silicon $AMD, $AVGO, $QCOM photonics bottlenecks $AAOI, $COHR, $LITE, $TSEM, $AEHR and capacity plays $NBIS, $IREN, $CRWV.

That’s the part I care about.

we’re past the “AI is exciting” phase.

more GPUs means more memory.

more agents means more CPU.

more scale means bandwidth becomes the choke point.

..and when hyperscalers inevitably lag demand, neoclouds step in.

Mag7 gets the headlines.

I’d rather be where the dollars actually land.
